---
title: "Bayesian estimation supersedes the $t$ test"
date: today
---

## Introduction

::: {.callout-important}
## Bayesian inference

Bayesian inference is merely the reallocation of credibility across a space of candidate possibilities.
:::

In Bayesian estimation, we reallocate belief toward the parameter values that are consistent with the data.

## Descriptive Model

1. Specify a descriptive model for the data

$$
\begin{align}
y_{i} \mid k &\sim \mathrm{N}(\mu_{k}, \sigma_{k}^{2}) \\
i &= 1, \ldots, N \\
k &= 1, \ldots, K
\end{align}
$$

- $\mu_{1} - \mu_{2}$: differences between central tendencies,
- $\sigma_{1} - \sigma_{2}$: difference between variabilities
